mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound for an extended period keeps the equity discount rate compressed and cost of capital low, supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as accommodative policy continues.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 as of 2021-06, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX exhibits negative median forward returns during sustained near-zero rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in the unemployment rate crossing a defined improvement threshold signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises expected consumer income and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points over the trailing 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.9 as of 2021-06, declining from pandemic peak
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following UNRATE-improvement triggers are not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) across a 2-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts jointly signal broad-based consumer and residential investment demand strength that exceeds what either series alone would imply, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; the interaction captures reinforcing demand-side momentum not present when only one series rises.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=620214 and HOUST=1572 as of mid-2021 reflecting reopening demand surge
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS-HOUST positive momentum periods fail to produce forward 20-day returns significantly above the marginal single-series-conditioned baseline, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not higher than either univariate IC.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle industrial recovery, sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect restocking and capacity utilization gains that feed through to corporate revenue growth, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, but this relationship is specific to recovery regimes and does not hold in late-cycle or contraction phases.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=100.0951 as of 2021-06, index still below pre-pandemic supply capacity
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrend fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) once industrial production surpasses its pre-recession peak, or the effect reverses sign outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation crosses above a threshold indicating overheating price pressure, markets price in higher probability of future monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=270.981 as of 2021-06 with inflation running well above target
regime_hint: rising inflation with policy tightening anticipation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances when CPI YoY exceeds the 5% threshold, or Rank IC is not negative and significant over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll employment growth and unemployment rate decline provide a reinforcing confirmation of genuine labor market tightening rather than noisy single-indicator movement, generating a short-horizon positive equity reaction as investors reprice growth expectations within days of the joint signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=145759 thousand and UNRATE=5.9 as of 2021-06 both showing recovery trajectory
regime_hint: any regime with active labor market recovery
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down signal fails to produce 5-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional 5-day mean by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth trend in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue capacity, which is incorporated gradually into equity prices, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=19086.375 billion as of Q1 2021, above pre-pandemic level and rising
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, accounting for GDP revision lag.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains pinned near zero while CPI inflation simultaneously exceeds a high threshold, the resulting policy-inflation mismatch signals a heightened risk of abrupt future tightening (taper risk), which increases equity risk premia and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month; this interaction effect is distinct from either series acting alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 alongside CPIAUCSL=270.981 in mid-2021 indicating a growing policy-inflation gap
regime_hint: extended near-zero rate regime with rising inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered joint-condition instances, or the interaction term Rank IC is not more negative than either univariate FEDFUNDS or CPIAUCSL signal over a 2-year rolling window.
